findings.evidence: `rg` shows existing `code/TauCeti/FieldTheory/SquareClassGroup.lean:35: abbrev SquareClassGroup ...`, while the new module documents it as the same construction at `TauCeti/Algebra/Group/ElementaryTwoQuotient.lean:20-21` and exposes `abbrev ElementaryTwoQuotient` at line 55.
findings.fix: Keep the generic construction internal to the class-group wrapper, or refactor the existing `SquareClassGroup` to share this definition/equivalence so TauCeti does not expose two parallel public square-class quotient types.
findings.issue: `TauCeti.ElementaryTwoQuotient` publicly creates a second square-class quotient API for `G = Kˣ`, distinct from the existing `TauCeti.SquareClassGroup`, even though the roadmap target is the class-group quotient.

findings.evidence: The exported operation lemmas are only `elementaryTwoQuotientMk_mul`, `elementaryTwoQuotientMk_one`, and `elementaryTwoQuotientMk_prod` (`TauCeti/NumberTheory/ClassGroup/ElementaryTwoQuotient.lean:59-73`); the equality criterion itself uses division at lines 80-83.
findings.fix: Add class-group and generic `mk_inv`/`mk_div` characteristic lemmas, with appropriate `[simp]` annotations if they normalize to neg/sub consistently with `mk_mul` and `mk_one`.
findings.issue: The class-group API states equality via `IsSquare (C / D)` but exposes no `elementaryTwoQuotientMk_inv` or `elementaryTwoQuotientMk_div` lemmas, so downstream divisor/class manipulations must unfold to the `Additive`/`ModN` implementation.

summary: The PR exposes a duplicate general quotient surface while leaving the class-group quotient short of the operation lemmas needed to use the stated API without unfolding.
